_comment: Flavor text for Sigh's Hit Attribution module. Each top-level key is a layer type. Each layer has a damage-agnostic 'default' array plus one entry per damage type. A damage-type entry is either a flat array (range-agnostic) or an object with 'any' (range-agnostic), 'melee', and 'ranged' arrays; 'melee'/'ranged' is preferred when the attack's range is known, falling back to 'any', then to the layer's 'default'. One line is chosen at random from the resolved pool. Tokens: {attacker} {attack} {defender} {armorName} {shieldName} {spellName} {dexMod} {abilityMod} {abilityAbbr} {vs} {ves} {isAre} {wasWere} {dtAdj}. {dtAdj} is a random damage-type-appropriate adjective drawn from the top-level 'adjectives' bank (keyed by damage type) — use it instead of hardcoding a specific adjective so repeated templates don't all read identically. Keep bank entries consonant-starting so 'a {dtAdj}' never needs to become 'an {dtAdj}'.

default: {attacker}'s {attack} sail{vs} hopelessly wide and {isAre} never going to land.
{attacker}'s {attack} miss{ves} completely, connecting with nothing but air.
A clumsy {attack} from {attacker} find{vs} nothing but empty space.
{attacker} overextend{vs} on the {attack} and nearly loses their footing entirely.
{attacker} commits fully to the {attack} and finds only empty space for the effort.
The {attack} never had a real angle on {defender} to begin with.
{attacker}'s footing falters mid-swing, and the {attack} {wasWere} doomed from the start.
Not even close. {defender} didn't need to move a muscle.

any: {attacker}'s {attack} go{vs} wide, finding nothing but empty air.
The {attack} from {attacker} {isAre} telegraphed a mile away; {defender} barely needed to move.
{attacker} lunge{vs} with {attack} but the angle is all wrong from the start.
{attacker}'s {dtAdj} {attack} sail{vs} hopelessly wide of {defender}.
The razor-tipped puncture of {attacker}'s {attack} amounts to nothing; {defender} is never even threatened.
{attacker} overcommits on a {dtAdj} {attack}, and the jab goes nowhere near {defender}.
The point behind {attacker}'s {attack} fizzles out long before it reaches {defender}.
{attacker}'s aim is off from the start, and the {dtAdj} {attack} find{vs} only empty air.
A stray jab is all that's left of {attacker}'s {attack}; {defender} was never in danger.
melee: {attacker} overcommits at close range, and the {dtAdj} {attack} whiff{vs} entirely.
Standing right in front of {defender}, {attacker}'s {dtAdj} {attack} still find{vs} nothing.
The {dtAdj} {attack} swing{vs} wide at arm's length; {defender} doesn't even flinch.
Point-blank, and {attacker} still misses; the {attack}'s point finds nothing but air.
{attacker} lunges in with a {dtAdj} {attack}, but there's nothing to hit at this range.
The {dtAdj} {attack} clip{vs} empty air right in front of {defender}'s face.
Even at arm's length, {attacker}'s {dtAdj} {attack} manage{vs} to miss entirely.
{attacker} telegraphs the {dtAdj} {attack} badly, and {defender} simply isn't there for it.
Close enough to touch, and the {dtAdj} {attack} still find{vs} nothing but empty space.
{attacker}'s footwork fails them mid-swing; the {dtAdj} {attack} never had a target.
ranged: The {dtAdj} {attack} sail{vs} clean over {defender}'s head from across the battlefield.
{attacker}'s aim drifts badly at range, and the {attack}'s puncture lands nowhere near {defender}.
Across the distance, the {dtAdj} {attack} veer{vs} wide of {defender} entirely.
{attacker}'s {attack} travel{vs} a long way; the point finds absolutely nothing.
The {dtAdj} {attack} lose{vs} momentum long before it reaches {defender}.
{attacker}'s aim wanders at range; the {dtAdj} {attack} sail{vs} well wide of {defender}.
From across the field, the {dtAdj} {attack} never had the right trajectory.
{attacker}'s {dtAdj} {attack} drop{vs} short, falling well shy of {defender}.
Distance punishes bad aim; the {dtAdj} {attack} find{vs} nothing but open ground.
{attacker} looses the {dtAdj} {attack} too early; it never had a chance of connecting.
